mappings.rationale: The primary VSI for this section, covering cell theory, cell classification, cell design and organization, the cell cycle, mitosis, meiosis, and fertilization.
Covers macromolecules in cells (nucleic acids, proteins, polysaccharides) and the molecular basis of cell design and genetic replication.
Addresses fertilization, cell division, and the cell cycle including how cells combine to form tissues and multicellular organisms.
Covers the essential tools for studying cell structure, including the morphological elements and organelles detailed in this section.
Addresses the molecular system of cells including nucleic acids, proteins, lipids, and small molecules that constitute cell design.
Directly relevant to cell division, differentiation, and the functional aspects of cells forming tissues and multicellular organisms.
Covers chromosomes, genetic replication, and the molecular basis of the cell cycle including mitosis and meiosis.
Addresses procaryotic cell structure and classification, comparing procaryotic and eucaryotic cells as outlined in the section.
Covers procaryotic cell structure and binary fission as a form of cell reproduction, relevant to cell theory and classification.
